description: Full-featured dashboard covering every collector pgxporter ships data for: activity, throughput, cache, locks, WAL, checkpointer, replication, storage, tables, exporter self-metrics.

panels.title: Total cardinality
panels.description: Sum of per-collector emitted series. Spikes here precede Prometheus ingest pressure.

targets.expr: sum by (datname) (rate(pg_stat_database_blks_hit_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", datname=~"$datname", datname!=""}[5m])) / (sum by (datname) (rate(pg_stat_database_blks_hit_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", datname=~"$datname", datname!=""}[5m])) + sum by (datname) (rate(pg_stat_database_blks_read_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", datname=~"$datname", datname!=""}[5m])))

panels.title: pg_stat_io — read bytes/sec by backend_type (PG 16+)
panels.description: Reads/sec × op_bytes (block size, ~8192). Empty on PG < 16.

targets.expr: sum by (backend_type) (rate(pg_stat_io_hits_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[5m])) / (sum by (backend_type) (rate(pg_stat_io_hits_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[5m])) + sum by (backend_type) (rate(pg_stat_io_reads_total{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance"}[5m])))

panels.title: Temp files / sec & temp bytes / sec
panels.description: Non-zero indicates work_mem spilling to disk — usually a sort/hash-join the planner couldn't fit in memory.

panels.title: Bgwriter buffers (alloc/clean) per sec
panels.description: In PG 17+ the checkpoint/buffers-written counters moved from pg_stat_bgwriter to pg_stat_checkpointer — see the next panel.

panels.title: Replication slots — retained WAL bytes
panels.description: WAL the primary cannot recycle until the slot's consumer catches up. Unbounded growth here is the classic 'forgotten replication slot fills the disk' incident.

panels.title: WAL receiver — flush vs latest_end (replica side)
Top 10 tables by sequential-scan tuple reads
Time since last (auto)vacuum (seconds)
panels.description: Empty until a row has actually been (auto)vacuumed once. A relentlessly-rising line on a high-churn table is the canonical autovacuum-can't-keep-up signal.

targets.expr: time() - max by (datname, schemaname, relname) (pg_stat_user_tables_last_autovacuum{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", datname=~"$datname", datname!=""})
time() - max by (datname, schemaname, relname) (pg_stat_user_tables_last_vacuum{job=~"$job", instance=~"$instance", datname=~"$datname", datname!=""})

panels.title: Exporter self-metrics (see pgxporter-health.json for full detail)

title: pgxporter — Postgres (full)
